title: PR review loop: detect and fix broken CI
description: The pr-review-loop skill currently ignores CI failures. After commit-and-push, it triggers the next review without checking CI. Need: (1) check-ci.sh script to wait for CI and report failures, (2) SKILL.md update to add CI fix loop between push and next review trigger.
close_reason: Added check-ci.sh script and updated SKILL.md with CI failure handling loop

title: pr-review-loop: add terraform + rails + javascript language templates
description:
## Why

98b shipped golang.md + python.md. Three more languages are common enough in production repos that they deserve first-party templates: terraform (IaC, almost always coexists with a service-language pack), rails (one of the most opinionated frameworks), and javascript/typescript (covering both with no-pushy-TS-migration stance).

## Decisions (from 2026-05-20 session)

- **Terraform**: standalone language pack. Per-subtree install from 98b handles the typical "Terraform in /terraform/" subdir case. Manifest markers: main.tf / variables.tf / outputs.tf / versions.tf / providers.tf in a directory (any one indicates an active Terraform module).
- **JS/TS**: single combined `javascript.md` pack. Triggered by package.json. Reviewers handle both JS and TS code. The no-pushy-conversion rule lives in a `## js-vs-ts-policy-reviewer` agent that explicitly does NOT propose JS → TS migration. TS-specific reviewers (no-`any`, strict-mode) only fire when tsconfig.json is detected (per-reviewer scope inside the template).
- **Rails**: full pack. Manifest: Gemfile (and contains `gem "rails"`). Reviewers cover the patterns that bite Rails repos most: test-coverage, n+1, strong-params, migration-safety, callback-overuse, mass-assignment, route-bloat.

## Scope of changes (single PR)

1. **`agent-reviewer-templates/`** — three new files (terraform.md, rails.md, javascript.md), authored from scratch (vendor-agnostic; not 521-specific).
2. **`detect-language.sh`** — new manifest detectors:
   - terraform: any of main.tf / variables.tf / outputs.tf / versions.tf / providers.tf in a directory
   - rails: Gemfile with `gem "rails"` mentioned (open Gemfile to verify; raw Gemfile alone is ambiguous — could be Sinatra or other)
   - javascript: package.json (with or without tsconfig.json)
3. **`install-template.sh`** — no change needed; accepts any lang via TEMPLATES_DIR lookup
4. **Tests** — extend test-language-templates.sh with detection cases for the three new languages (single + polyglot scenarios) and install cases
5. **Plugin bump** — 1.4.0 → 1.5.0; AGENT-REVIEWERS.md root pin bumped
6. **Templates' own `defaults_version_checked`** — pinned to 1.5.0 (install-template overrides at write-time but source consistency matters per PR #26)

## Out of scope

- Audit-agents (q2h) integration — separate ticket
- The Project-Specific Reviewer Recommendations flow (dbs, shipped in 98b) is unchanged
- Subtree terraform-reviewer in existing language packs (NOT adding terraform-reviewer H2 to golang.md/python.md — the standalone terraform.md handles that case via per-subtree install)
close_reason: Closed

title: pr-review-loop: plan-mode recommendation pass for project-specific reviewers (98b tranche d)
description:
Part of 98b. Fourth tranche, depends on (c). Also relates to q2h (audit-agents) — both should call the same recommendation code path.

## Plan-mode pass
- After template install completes (or on standalone request), enter Claude Code's plan mode via ExitPlanMode flow
- Read the repo structure, key files, and .beads/ history to characterize the codebase
- Propose additional `# Agents` entries that fit the project (e.g., migration-safety-reviewer for a DB-heavy repo, prompt-injection-reviewer for an LLM-heavy repo)
- Present as a plan the user can accept/modify/reject
- On accept: append to the appropriate AGENT-REVIEWERS.md (root, or matching subtree if the proposed agent is scoped)
- On reject/skip: leave the template-only install in place

## Feasibility check (gate before implementation)
- Verify Claude Code's plan mode can actually be entered mid-skill-execution from inside the pr-review-loop. If not, design a TodoWrite-based proposed-agents-review surface as a fallback.
- If plan mode IS usable, document the entry shape in SKILL.md so the agent has a consistent starting point.

## Shared with q2h
- This is the same 'suggest additional agents for this codebase' code path q2h needs. Coordinate the recommendation prompt template so both surfaces produce consistent suggestions.
notes:
## Design pivot (2026-05-20, after feasibility check)

**Plan mode is the wrong primitive.** Verified via claude-code-guide:
- `EnterPlanMode` exists and agents can call it, but it's effectively user-initiated — the user must drive the exit
- `ExitPlanMode` returns no structured data (binary "approved" signal only)
- A skill cannot programmatically read back which items the user approved/modified/rejected

**New design: AskUserQuestion (multi-select).**

Flow:
1. Skill reads the repo (Glob/Read/Grep) to characterize the codebase — no mode switch needed; reads are non-mutating in normal mode
2. Skill generates 3-7 proposed repo-specific reviewers
3. Skill presents the proposals via AskUserQuestion with `multiSelect: true`
   - ≤4 proposals → single question
   - 5-7 proposals → two consecutive questions (first 4, then the rest)
   - Each option shows the proposed reviewer name + one-line focus + "where it applies" (file scope)
4. Skill appends user-accepted proposals to the appropriate AGENT-REVIEWERS.md file
   - Root-scoped reviewers → root AGENT-REVIEWERS.md
   - Subtree-scoped (e.g., "migration-safety-reviewer" only for files in /db) → subtree AGENT-REVIEWERS.md
5. Bumps defaults_version_checked if the merged config block changed

**What this loses vs. plan mode**: no "modify before accept" step. User accepts the proposal verbatim or rejects it. If they want to edit, they accept then edit AGENT-REVIEWERS.md by hand after. Trade-off: simpler implementation, fewer round-trips. Modify-before-accept can be a follow-up if dogfood shows it's needed.

## Updated scope

1. **SKILL.md** — new `### Project-Specific Reviewer Recommendations` section
2. **Proposal-generation Task prompt** — included in SKILL.md as a template so the agent has a consistent starting point. Reads CLAUDE.md, package manifests, sample of source files, .beads/ history if present. Outputs a structured list of 3-7 proposed reviewers.
3. **No new scripts** — the proposal generation runs as a Task; the writing-to-AGENT-REVIEWERS.md happens via Edit/Write tools on the appropriate file. install-template.sh from tranche a already handles the merged config update via re-invocation if needed.
4. **No new tests** — the proposal-generation logic is LLM-driven, not deterministic. Validate via dogfood.

## Cross-reference fix needed in tranche c (kxi)

kxi shipped with a forward reference to "Project-Specific Reviewer Recommendations" section. Tranche d adds that section. The reference will resolve naturally once this lands.
close_reason: Closed

title: pr-review-loop: detect-language.sh + install-template.sh + tests (98b tranche a)
description:
Part of 98b. First tranche.

## detect-language.sh
- Scan repo for language manifest markers (go.mod, pyproject.toml, setup.py, Cargo.toml, Gemfile, package.json+tsconfig.json, etc.)
- Emit JSON list of `{language, subtree}` pairs (subtree = directory holding the manifest, relative to repo root)
- Multi-instance: a Go workspace with go.mod in /serviceA and /serviceB emits two Go entries
- Deterministic — no LLM inference
- One detector per shipping template (initial: go, python)

## install-template.sh
- Args: `<language>:<subtree>` pairs (e.g., `golang:backend python:frontend`)
- For each pair, copy the language template to `<subtree>/AGENT-REVIEWERS.md`
- Merge all packs' `# Configuration` blocks into a single ROOT AGENT-REVIEWERS.md:
  - Union of `disabled` lists
  - Merge `overlap_acknowledged` maps
  - Single `defaults_version_checked` pinned to current plugin version
- Each per-subtree file gets ONLY `# Agents` + `# Guidelines` (no `# Configuration` — root owns config)
- Idempotent: if AGENT-REVIEWERS.md already exists at a subtree path, refuse and instruct the user (script must NOT silently overwrite)

## Tests
- detect-language.sh: fixtures with single-language, polyglot subtrees, same-dir polyglot, no-match, nested manifests
- install-template.sh: single-pack root install, two-pack per-subtree install, config-merge correctness, refuse-on-existing behaviour
close_reason: Closed

title: pr-review-loop: language-aware default AGENT-REVIEWERS.md templates with copy-and-extend offer
description:
## Pain point

The plugin ships 6 default specialist reviewer agents (bfd) but no project-level `AGENT-REVIEWERS.md` template. Projects that don't already have one get the defaults but miss out on language-specific reviewers (Go idioms, Python type hygiene, Rust borrow patterns, etc.). The user has started authoring language-keyed defaults (golang, python so far) and wants the loop to offer them automatically when it can detect a language match.

## What to add

### 1. Ship language-keyed AGENT-REVIEWERS.md templates

A new directory in the plugin: `plugins/pr-review-loop/agent-reviewer-templates/<language>.md`. Each file is a complete starter AGENT-REVIEWERS.md with language-specific `# Agents` (e.g., Go's `idiomatic-go-reviewer`, Python's `type-hint-reviewer`, etc.) plus an empty `# Configuration` block with `defaults_version_checked` pinned to the shipping plugin version.

Initial set: `golang.md`, `python.md` (designed by user already). Additional languages added over time (typescript, rust, ruby, java, etc.).

Each template's `# Agents` should be additive on top of the 6 baked-in defaults — language reviewers complement, not replace, code-reviewer / silent-failure-hunter / etc.

### 2. Language detection (deterministic, file-based)

When the loop runs and finds NO AGENT-REVIEWERS.md anywhere in the repo, run language detection on the repo root + a sample of changed files:

| Language | Markers (any match counts) |
|----------|----------------------------|
| Go | `go.mod`, `*.go` files in diff |
| Python | `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, `requirements.txt`, `*.py` in diff |
| TypeScript | `tsconfig.json`, `*.ts`/`*.tsx` in diff |
| Rust | `Cargo.toml`, `*.rs` in diff |
| Ruby | `Gemfile`, `*.rb` in diff |
| (extensible — one detector per template shipped) |

Detection happens at pre-loop setup (after discover-agents.sh confirms no user AGENT-REVIEWERS.md exists). Multi-language repos can match multiple templates.

### 3. Copy-and-extend offer flow

If language detection matches one or more templates, BEFORE round 1, prompt the user:

> "No AGENT-REVIEWERS.md found. Detected: Go (go.mod), Python (pyproject.toml).
> 
> Available templates:
>   [1] golang.md — adds idiomatic-go-reviewer, error-wrapping-reviewer
>   [2] python.md — adds type-hint-reviewer, dataclass-vs-namedtuple-reviewer
> 
> Copy templates to AGENT-REVIEWERS.md? [y/N/select]"

- **y** (single language detected): copy the one template, then enter plan mode (step 4 below)
- **y** (multiple languages detected): copy ALL matching templates merged into one AGENT-REVIEWERS.md (deduplicate `# Configuration`, concat `# Agents` sections), then enter plan mode
- **select**: interactive picker for multi-match case
- **N**: skip; proceed to round 1 with just the 6 baked-in defaults; the offer is recorded as declined for this loop invocation so it doesn't fire again mid-loop

### 4. Plan-mode recommendation pass (after copy or instead of copy)

After the template is copied (or if the user declined the copy but wants tailored suggestions), enter Claude Code's **plan mode** (via ExitPlanMode flow) to:

1. Read the repo structure, key files, and `.beads/` history (if present) to understand the codebase's character
2. Suggest additional `# Agents` entries that would fit — these are project-specific reviewers the templates can't anticipate (e.g., a `migration-safety-reviewer` for a repo with heavy DB migrations, a `prompt-injection-reviewer` for an LLM-heavy repo)
3. Present the proposed agents as a plan the user can accept, modify, or reject
4. On accept: append the new agents to AGENT-REVIEWERS.md's `# Agents` section
5. On reject or skip: leave the template-only version in place

The plan-mode pass is OPT-IN — the prompt shape is:

> "Template copied. Want recommendations for repo-specific reviewers based on the codebase? [y/N]"

This is the bridge into audit-agents-style behavior at first-time setup, complementing q2h (which handles already-customized repos).

## Why this design

- **Template files in the plugin**: version-controlled with the plugin, get the same SemVer treatment as the 6 baked-in defaults, can be reviewed in the plugin's own dogfood loop
- **Detection at pre-loop setup, not first-PR**: surfaces the offer at the natural moment (user just ran the loop) without polluting unrelated workflows
- **Copy then offer plan mode (two-stage)**: lets the user accept the conservative path (just templates) or invest more time in the tailored path. Avoids the failure mode where plan mode produces too many suggestions and the user gives up.
- **Multi-language merge**: real projects rarely use exactly one language; the loop should support that without forcing the user to manually merge templates

## Scope of changes

1. **`plugins/pr-review-loop/agent-reviewer-templates/<lang>.md`** — initial files for golang and python (user has drafts). Add a contribution doc explaining the template shape.
2. **`scripts/detect-language.sh`** — pure-bash file-marker detection. Returns a JSON list of matched languages. No LLM inference (deterministic for testability).
3. **`scripts/discover-agents.sh`** — when no AGENT-REVIEWERS.md is found, call `detect-language.sh`. Surface the result in `discover-agents.sh`'s output under a new `language_detection` block alongside `configuration`.
4. **`scripts/install-template.sh`** — given one or more language names, merge their templates into a single AGENT-REVIEWERS.md at repo root. Bumps `defaults_version_checked` to current plugin version.
5. **`SKILL.md`** — document the pre-loop language-detection step and the two-stage offer (copy + plan mode). Slot the new step into Pre-Loop Setup between "Discover and merge agents" and "Check the stale pin" (since stale pin is irrelevant when there's no config yet).
6. **Plan-mode integration** — the SKILL.md instruction for the loop to enter plan mode after template copy. Plan mode prompt template provided in SKILL.md so the agent has a consistent starting point.
7. **Tests** — `detect-language.sh` (input fixtures with various marker combinations), `install-template.sh` (single-language, multi-language merge, dedup of `# Configuration`).
8. **Relationship to q2h (audit-agents)**: this ticket and q2h overlap at the plan-mode-recommendation step. Both should call the same underlying "suggest additional agents for this codebase" code path. Mark q2h as related; coordinate the recommendation prompt so both surfaces give consistent suggestions.

## Acceptance

- Plugin ships at minimum `golang.md` + `python.md` templates (more added incrementally)
- `detect-language.sh` returns a deterministic, testable list of matched languages
- When `discover-agents.sh` finds no AGENT-REVIEWERS.md AND detects a matching language, the loop surfaces the copy-and-extend offer before round 1
- On user accept: template(s) merged into a new AGENT-REVIEWERS.md with `defaults_version_checked` set to current plugin version
- Plan-mode offer fires after copy (or on standalone request), produces structured agent suggestions the user can accept/reject
- Multi-language detection handled (merged AGENT-REVIEWERS.md, no duplicates)
- Tests cover detection + template installation + dedup edge cases
- Bump pr-review-loop plugin minor — feature add, no breaking change for existing-config repos

## Related work

- **bfd** (closed, shipped at 0000990): the 6 baked-in default specialist agents that templates extend
- **q2h** (open): audit-agents subcommand — handles the "user already has AGENT-REVIEWERS.md, recommend improvements" path. This ticket handles the complementary "no AGENT-REVIEWERS.md yet, offer language template" path. The plan-mode recommendation step is shared infrastructure both should use.
- **xyf** (open): bots config block — touches AGENT-REVIEWERS.md format. Sequencing: ship the template content with whatever's in `# Configuration` at the time; templates need bumping when the config schema bumps (same as user-authored configs do today via the stale-pin gate)

## Out of scope

- Auto-detecting language from `git log` history rather than file markers (too noisy; markers are the right signal)
- Multi-language merge UI (custom picker, ordering, etc.) — v1 is "all matched languages merged, deterministic order"
- Template parameterization (e.g., variable substitution) — v1 templates are static files
- Non-language template categories (e.g., a "monorepo" template, a "library vs application" template) — those can be a follow-up if there's demand
notes:
## Design decisions (2026-05-20 session)

**Multi-language: per-subtree install** chosen over single-root variants.
- `detect-language.sh` returns WHERE each manifest lives (subtree path), not just IF a language is present
- Each pack installs at the subtree's AGENT-REVIEWERS.md (e.g., `/backend/AGENT-REVIEWERS.md`)
- Existing hierarchical scoping handles file-type filtering and per-scope agent visibility
- No same-name collisions (the 8 shared reviewer names live in different files)
- Same-directory polyglot is the rare-edge fallback → user pick-one prompt

**# Configuration handling**
- Only root config is honored per current spec
- `install-template.sh` merges each pack's `# Configuration` into a single root file:
  - Union of `disabled` (both packs disable same 3 defaults anyway — silent-failure-hunter, comment-analyzer, code-simplifier)
  - Merge `overlap_acknowledged` maps (both packs have same overlap entries for shared reviewers)
  - Single `defaults_version_checked` pinned to current plugin version

**Spec reshape (Option A) confirmed**
- Templates renamed H1: `# Code Review Agents — <Lang>` → `# Agents`
- Pack metadata sub-sections (How the pack runs / Tooling assumed in CI / Severity convention / Output format / Deferring findings with beads) moved to `# Guidelines`
- Priorities shifted P0/P1/P2 → P1/P2/P3 (P1+P2 still = blocking, P3 = nitpick — matches qrc Priority Mapping)
- Templates shipped at `plugins/pr-review-loop/agent-reviewer-templates/{golang,python}.md` (committed in this session)

## Tranche split (4 child tickets)
- (a) detect-language.sh + install-template.sh scripts + tests
- (b) discover-agents.sh integration + spawning-summary surfacing
- (c) SKILL.md docs (offer + copy + plan-mode)
- (d) plan-mode recommendation pass

All four ship in ONE PR per user direction. Tickets are for tracking, not separate PRs.
close_reason: Closed

title: pr-review-loop: configurable bot-source enablement (skip Gemini/Cursor/Claude polling when repo doesn't use them)
description:
## Pain point

The loop unconditionally polls Gemini (`get-review-comments.sh --wait`) for up to 5 minutes per F6, plus the initial C1 wait. If Gemini isn't connected to the repo, every round burns 5 minutes of dead time. Same problem for Cursor / Claude bot if those aren't configured.

There's currently no way to short-circuit this. The `--skip-stale-check` flag is the only top-level loop knob; there's no per-bot enable/disable.

## What to add

A `bots` block in `# Configuration` (AGENT-REVIEWERS.md), composed C+E-style with the existing config:

```json
{
  "bots": {
    "gemini": true,
    "cursor": false,
    "claude": false
  }
}
```

**Defaults: all `false` (opt-in).** The `bots` config controls whether the loop **invites the next round of reviews** from each bot, NOT whether it processes existing reviews. Pure-agent repos that don't configure anything get agent-only review with no dead Gemini polling.

## Critical distinction: addressing vs inviting

The loop must always **address** any reviews it *sees* — from any bot, regardless of `bots` config. If Gemini posted a review because a human ran `/gemini review` manually or the bot was active before the config flipped, those comments still need replies. The config only governs whether the loop *invites* the bot to participate in the next round.

Per-step impact:

| Step | Bot config controls | Always runs |
|------|---------------------|-------------|
| C1 (fetch Gemini comments) | `--wait` flag (only `--wait` if `bots.gemini=true`) | non-blocking fetch — always processes what's there |
| C2 (fetch other bot comments) | `--wait` flag (only `--wait` if `bots.cursor` or `bots.claude` is true) | non-blocking fetch — always processes what's there |
| F1 (reply to Gemini) | nothing — always replies if there are comments | yes |
| F2 (reply to other bots) | nothing — always replies if there are comments | yes |
| F6 (trigger next review) | **gated** — only calls `trigger-review.sh --gemini` / `--cursor` / `--claude` for bots where config is `true` | F6 itself always runs |

The Spawning Summary surfaces which bots are being *invited* (third line: "Inviting: gemini" or "Inviting: none — agent-only loop").

Agent-only mode (the new default, all bots false) means: agents + address any drive-by bot comments that happen to land + don't invite bots to the next round.

## Drive-by detection: offer to flip config when bot evidence appears

When C1 / C2 fetches return comments from a bot whose `bots.<name>` is currently `false`, the loop has observed evidence that bot IS actually active on the repo. In that case, **before proceeding to BATCH POINT**, the loop offers to update the config:

> "Detected a Gemini review on this PR, but `bots.gemini` is `false` in AGENT-REVIEWERS.md. This means the loop addressed Gemini's comments but won't invite it for the next round. Update config to `bots.gemini: true` so future rounds invite Gemini automatically? [y/N]"

If the user accepts:
- The loop edits AGENT-REVIEWERS.md to set `bots.gemini: true` (or adds the `bots` block if missing)
- Bumps `defaults_version_checked` to the current plugin version (since the user is making an active config change, the pin is fresh)
- Includes the AGENT-REVIEWERS.md edit in the round's F4 commit (clearly marked in the commit body: "Auto-config: bots.gemini -> true after detecting Gemini review")

If the user declines:
- Note the decision in this-loop-invocation state so the prompt doesn't fire again on every round
- Continue the loop normally (bot was addressed; not invited next round)

This makes the config self-healing. New PRs in a Gemini-connected repo flip the config on first observation; pure-agent repos never see the prompt.

## Why all-false is the right default

- The plugin ships its 6 default specialist agents — that's the baseline review surface; bots are augmentation
- Most repos don't have all three bots installed; the current "always invite" default is the bug we're fixing
- Opt-in matches the convention for other integration features (`independent_validator.skip_for`, `disabled` defaults list)
- Migration path is clean: existing user repos hit the stale-pin gate AND get the drive-by detection prompt on the first round that surfaces a bot comment

## Migration

Existing user repos (like `devon-claude-skills` itself) have Gemini configured and rely on inviting it each round. When this lands:

1. `defaults_version_checked` becomes stale for those repos
2. Stale-pin check fires; user runs `/pr-review-loop:audit-agents`
3. `audit-agents` (q2h) detects `.gemini/config.yaml` or recent Gemini comments and recommends `bots.gemini: true` at audit-time
4. **Secondary safety net**: if the user skips audit-agents and re-runs the loop with `--skip-stale-check`, the drive-by detection will fire on the first round where a Gemini comment shows up — same outcome via a different path
5. Pin bumps; loop resumes inviting Gemini

## Scope of changes

1. **`_parse_configuration.sh`** — validate the `bots` block: must be an object, known keys only (gemini/cursor/claude), all values bool. Reject `{ "bots": "no" }`, warn on unknown keys.
2. **`discover-agents.sh`** — surface resolved `bots` in the configuration output (defaults filled in to all-false via `select(type=="object" and has(...))` pattern, same as `independent_validator` from ydy).
3. **`SKILL.md`** — document `bots` config; clarify the "address vs invite" distinction in the C1/C2/F6 step text; document the drive-by detection prompt; spawning summary documents "Inviting: ..." line; add an "agent-only mode" note.
4. **F6 logic in SKILL.md** — F6 currently always calls `trigger-review.sh --gemini --wait`. Update to: "for each bot where `bots.<name> = true`, trigger it. If no bots are invited, F6 is a no-op (the loop relies on C3 agent state for round signal)."
5. **C1/C2 wait-flag logic** — C1's `--wait` becomes conditional on `bots.gemini`. C2's `--wait` becomes conditional on cursor/claude. The non-wait fetch always runs.
6. **Drive-by detection (new C1/C2 sub-step)** — after fetching comments, if any comments are from a bot where `bots.<name> = false`, surface the offer-to-flip prompt. Track per-invocation "user declined for X" so we don't ask twice.
7. **Auto-config write helper (new script)** — `scripts/update-bots-config.sh <bot-name> <true|false>` edits AGENT-REVIEWERS.md's `# Configuration` JSON block to flip the value; idempotent; bumps `defaults_version_checked` too. F4 picks up the edit and includes it in the round commit.
8. **Tests** — bots-block validation cases (Tests 23-26-ish): valid all-true, valid mixed, default all-false when block absent, invalid non-object, invalid value type, unknown-key warning. Tests for `update-bots-config.sh` (adds block when missing, flips key when present, bumps pin).
9. **`audit-agents` tool** (separate ticket q2h, but flag here): detect `.gemini/config.yaml`, existing bot comments on recent PRs, recommend the corresponding `bots.*: true`.

## Edge cases

- **All bots false (the new default)**: agent-only mode. F6 is a no-op. C1/C2 still run as fast non-wait fetches in case bot comments exist.
- **Drive-by detected, user declines flip**: remember decision for this loop invocation; do not re-prompt in subsequent rounds of the same loop. New `bd remember` entry? No — ephemeral per-invocation state, fine to keep in TodoWrite.
- **Drive-by detected, user accepts flip mid-loop**: the AGENT-REVIEWERS.md edit happens before F4; F4 commit includes both the review fixes and the config edit. Next round's F6 will invite the newly-enabled bot.
- **`bots` block missing entirely**: defaults to all `false`. Existing user repos hit the stale-pin gate and/or drive-by prompt.

## Acceptance

- `# Configuration .bots` block parsed + validated
- Defaults to all-false when block is absent
- C1 / C2 always fetch existing comments (no `--wait` if corresponding bot is disabled)
- F1 / F2 always reply to any comments present, regardless of `bots` config
- F6 only invites bots where `bots.<name> = true`; F6 is a no-op when all are false
- **Drive-by detection: when C1/C2 find comments from a bot whose `bots.<name>=false`, the loop offers to flip the config; the AGENT-REVIEWERS.md edit (if accepted) lands in F4's commit**
- Pre-loop spawning summary surfaces invited bots (third line: "Inviting: gemini" or "Inviting: none — agent-only loop")
- Tests cover the skip paths, validation cases, default-false-when-absent, and the `update-bots-config.sh` helper
- `audit-agents` recommends enabling bots when corresponding config/comments are detected (track in q2h)
- Bump pr-review-loop plugin to 1.5.0 — stale-pin gate + drive-by detection both handle migration

## Out of scope

title: pr-review-loop: MCP-backed GitHub transport for Claude Cowork's restricted-VM environment (gh fallback)
description:
Add an MCP-backed alternative path for GitHub operations so pr-review-loop works in Claude Cowork's restricted VM (which blocks all network access except a few allowlisted endpoints). The MCP path coexists with — does not replace — the `gh` CLI default.

## Why this is needed now

Plugin install in Claude Cowork succeeded (after the marketplace.json + per-plugin plugin.json fixes), but the loop fails at runtime because Cowork's VM blocks direct network access to GitHub. The `gh api`, `gh pr view`, and similar calls in our scripts fall on their face. Claude Cowork routes outbound traffic through approved MCP servers; an official GitHub MCP server is the canonical access path in that environment.

## What this is NOT

This is NOT a reversal of bd memory `decision-pr-review-loop-sticks-with-gh-api`. That decision was about implementation choice when both options work — `gh` is more token-efficient. This ticket is about working AT ALL in a constrained environment where `gh` cannot reach github.com. Two different concerns:

- **Local / standard agentic env**: `gh` stays the default (token efficiency)
- **Cowork / restricted VM**: MCP is required because `gh` is blocked

The previously-closed ticket ypj (investigate `mcp__github_inline_comment` as replacement for gh scripts) was about replacing `gh` everywhere. That stays closed — we are NOT replacing, just adding a fallback.

## What to build

### 1. Transport-abstraction shim

A single dispatch layer that each script calls instead of `gh` directly. The shim picks a backend per the detection rules below:

- `scripts/_gh_dispatch.sh` (or per-script helper functions) — wraps every gh invocation we currently make: `gh pr view`, `gh pr diff`, `gh pr comment`, `gh api repos/...`, `gh pr checks`, `gh pr review`, etc.
- The shim takes the same call signatures we use today; existing scripts don't need to know which backend is active.

### 2. Backend detection

In priority order:

1. **Environment override** (explicit): `PR_REVIEW_LOOP_BACKEND=gh|mcp|auto` env var wins.
2. **Auto-detect** (default): if `gh --version` succeeds AND a test call (`gh api user` or similar with timeout < 3s) succeeds, use `gh`. Otherwise fall back to `mcp`.
3. **MCP availability check**: if neither `gh` works nor a GitHub MCP server is configured, fail loudly with a clear message.

### 3. MCP backend implementation

For each `gh` operation we use, map to the equivalent MCP tool call. Likely target: the official GitHub MCP server (`github.com/github/github-mcp-server` or similar) — confirm during implementation. Operations we need:

- Read: `gh pr view --json ...` → MCP `get_pull_request` / similar
- Read: `gh pr diff <PR>` → MCP `get_pull_request_files` / `get_pull_request_diff`
- Read: `gh api repos/.../pulls/<PR>/comments` → MCP list-review-comments
- Write: `gh pr comment` → MCP create-issue-comment
- Write: `gh api .../pulls/<PR>/reviews` (pending review batching from ticket 77j) → MCP create-pull-request-review
- Write: `gh pr review --approve|--request-changes|--comment` → MCP same as above
- Status: `gh pr checks` → MCP get-check-suites or similar

### 4. Cost-awareness logging

Cowork accepts that MCP costs more tokens than `gh`. The shim logs which backend served each call so we can measure overhead via gx4 telemetry once that lands.

### 5. Documentation

SKILL.md updated:
- New "Environment Compatibility" section explaining `gh` (default) vs MCP (Cowork / restricted-VM) backends
- How to force one or the other via env var
- Required setup: configuring the GitHub MCP server in Claude Cowork's mcp-servers list
- Expected token-cost delta (estimate after implementation)

### 6. Behavioral parity tests

Run a representative subset of loop operations against both backends on the same PR; assert outputs match (same comments fetched, same review posted, etc.). Replay-harness (bl7) can support this once it lands.

## Acceptance

- Loop runs to completion in Claude Cowork's restricted VM using the MCP backend
- Loop continues to work locally using `gh` (no regression)
- Backend selection is auto by default, env-overridable
- Each operation logs which backend served it
- SKILL.md documents both paths and the trade-offs
- bd memory `decision-pr-review-loop-sticks-with-gh-api` updated to note the Cowork-MCP carve-out (see also separate memory update below)

## Memory update

After this ticket, update the existing memory `decision-pr-review-loop-sticks-with-gh-api` to add: "Exception: Claude Cowork and similar restricted-VM environments require MCP because `gh` cannot reach github.com. The shim layer auto-detects and falls back. See ticket [this one] for details."

## Priority rationale

P2 — blocks one of the primary target environments (Cowork). Not P1 (loop works in every other environment) but not P3 either (real product need, not a polish item).

## Out of scope

- Replacing `gh` everywhere (still rejected per ypj)
- Building our own MCP server (we'll use the official one)
- Generalizing to other MCP-backed services (GitLab, etc.) — stretch goal, not in this ticket

title: Backport: quality-weighted exit condition + Hard Round Ceiling
description:
Backport the quality-weighted exit condition + Hard Round Ceiling, replacing our simpler "ONE MORE LOOP" formulation.

## What to lift

From downstream SKILL.md "ONE MORE LOOP Rule" and surrounding text:

- **Actionable feedback** definition: "feedback you actually fix — not 'Won't fix' responses, not nitpicks, not zero-comment rounds"
- **Unifying with stopping heuristics**: A "Won't fix" round satisfies the ONE MORE LOOP trigger
- **Reset condition refinement**: only P1/P2 fixes reset the final-verification counter; P3 nitpick fixes do NOT reset
- **Quality-weighted exit condition** (replaces our simple "no actionable feedback"):
  - (a) No P1/P2 findings in the last round (per priority mapping in ticket 2)
  - (b) Last two rounds had only nitpick-level findings OR zero actionable fixes
  - (c) No contradictions across rounds
  - OR: **Hard Round Ceiling** has fired — stop and ask the user regardless

## Why

Our current "exit when no actionable feedback" treats "Won't fix" responses ambiguously: technically they're actionable (you responded), but if the underlying finding is a real P1/P2, "Won't fix" doesn't resolve it. The quality-weighted formulation handles this correctly.

## Acceptance

- "ONE MORE LOOP Rule" subsection rewritten with the new formulation
- "Exit condition (quality-weighted)" subsection added with the (a)(b)(c) criteria
- Hard Round Ceiling text lifted (need to fetch its full definition from downstream — referenced but I didn't see it in the diff I read)
- Cross-references to priority mapping (ticket 2) wired
- "Stopping Heuristics" section updated to reference the new quality-weighted formulation
- Scrubbed per bd memory `downstream-bidirectional-sync-and-scrub-rule`
